// Code Generated by Sidekick is for learning and experimentation purposes only.
using Microsoft.Data.SqlClient;
using Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration;
using Newtonsoft.Json;
using System.Diagnostics;
using System.Text;
using System.Text.Json;
using System.Text.RegularExpressions;
using System.Xml.Linq;

namespace EntityAttribute_POC
{
    public class NuGetPackageUpdater
    {
        // ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
        // FIELDS & CONSTRUCTOR
        // ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

        private readonly EventData _data;
        private readonly string _projectPath;

        public NuGetPackageUpdater(EventData data, string projectPath)
        {
            _data = data;
            _projectPath = projectPath;
        }

        // ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
        // PUBLIC — IS ALREADY AT REQUESTED VERSION?
        // ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

        /// <summary>
        /// Reads the version directly from the .csproj XML — never calls
        /// "dotnet list", which requires a clean restore and crashes when
        /// the project has unresolved NU1605 conflicts.
        /// </summary>
        public bool IsPackageAlreadyAtRequestedVersion()
        {
            var currentVersion = ReadVersionFromCsproj(
                _projectPath, _data.packageName ?? "");

            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(currentVersion))
                return false;

            return string.Equals(
                currentVersion.Trim(),
                (_data.version ?? "").Trim(),
                StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
        }

        // ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
        // PUBLIC — UPDATE PACKAGE (FULL SAFE FLOW)
        // ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

        /// <summary>
        /// Safe update flow that handles NU1605 transitive-version conflicts
        /// automatically without crashing the pipeline:
        ///
        ///   Step 1  Write new version to .csproj  (--no-restore)
        ///   Step 2  Trial restore — capture output, never throw
        ///   Step 3  If NU1605 detected → parse conflicting packages
        ///   Step 4  Pin each conflict to its required version (--no-restore)
        ///   Step 5  Restore again — throw only if still broken
        ///   Step 6  Confirm resolved version via .csproj XML
        /// </summary>
        public void UpdatePackage()
        {
            if (IsPackageAlreadyAtRequestedVersion())
            {
                Console.WriteLine(
                    $"[NuGet] Package '{_data.packageName}' is already at " +
                    $"version '{_data.version}'. Skipping.");
                return;
            }

            // ── Step 1: Write new version — no restore yet ────────────────
            Console.WriteLine(
                $"[NuGet] Writing {_data.packageName} {_data.version} " +
                $"to .csproj (--no-restore)...");

            RunCommand(
                $"add \"{_projectPath}\" package {_data.packageName} " +
                $"--version {_data.version} --no-restore",
                "Failed to write package reference to .csproj");

            // ── Step 2: Trial restore ─────────────────────────────────────
            Console.WriteLine("[NuGet] Running trial restore...");
            var (restoreOk, restoreOutput) = TryRestore();

            if (!restoreOk)
            {
                // ── Step 3: Parse NU1605 conflicts ────────────────────────
                var conflicts = ParseDowngradeConflicts(restoreOutput);

                if (conflicts.Count == 0)
                {
                    // Not a version-conflict — surface the real error
                    throw new Exception(
                        $"Restore failed (non-downgrade error):\n{restoreOutput}");
                }

                Console.WriteLine(
                    $"[NuGet] Detected {conflicts.Count} transitive version " +
                    $"conflict(s). Auto-pinning...");

                // ── Step 4: Pin each conflicting dependency ────────────────
                foreach (var (pkg, requiredVersion) in conflicts)
                {
                    Console.WriteLine(
                        $"[NuGet]   Pinning: {pkg} → {requiredVersion}");
                    RunCommand(
                        $"add \"{_projectPath}\" package {pkg} " +
                        $"--version {requiredVersion} --no-restore",
                        $"Failed to pin transitive dependency: {pkg}");
                }

                // ── Step 5: Re-run restore after pinning ───────────────────
                Console.WriteLine(
                    "[NuGet] Re-running restore after conflict resolution...");
                var (secondOk, secondOutput) = TryRestore();

                if (!secondOk)
                    throw new Exception(
                        $"Restore still failing after auto-pin:\n{secondOutput}");
            }

            // ── Step 6: Verify .csproj reflects the requested version ─────
            var updatedPackages = new Dictionary<string, string>
            {
                [_data.packageName ?? ""] = _data.version ?? ""
            };

            var csprojVersion = ReadVersionFromCsproj(
                _projectPath, _data.packageName ?? "");

            if (!string.Equals(
                    csprojVersion, _data.version,
                    StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
            {
                Console.WriteLine(
                    $"[NuGet] ⚠ .csproj shows '{csprojVersion}' but " +
                    $"'{_data.version}' was requested. " +
                    $"Running dependency alignment...");

                AlignTransitiveDependencies(updatedPackages);

                var (finalOk, finalOut) = TryRestore();
                if (!finalOk)
                    throw new Exception(
                        $"Restore failed after alignment:\n{finalOut}");
            }

            Console.WriteLine("[NuGet] ✅ Package update complete.");
        }

        // ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
        // PUBLIC — FULL MIGRATION PIPELINE
        // ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

        /// <summary>
        /// Orchestrates all phases:
        ///   Phase 1  — Detect affected .cs files
        ///   Phase 2  — LLM code migration
        ///   Phase 2.5— LLM test generation + execution
        ///   Phase 3  — Build + LLM self-correction
        /// </summary>
        public async Task<MigrationPipelineResult> RunFullMigrationPipelineAsync(
            string fromVersion)
        {
            var result = new MigrationPipelineResult
            {
                PackageName = _data.packageName ?? "",
                FromVersion = fromVersion,
                ToVersion = _data.version ?? ""
            };

            var projectFolder = Path.GetDirectoryName(_projectPath)!;

            // ── Phase 1: Detect affected .cs files ───────────────────────────
            Console.WriteLine("\n════════════════════════════════════════");
            Console.WriteLine("  PHASE 1 — Detecting package usage");
            Console.WriteLine("════════════════════════════════════════");

            var repoRoot = GitChangeDetector.FindRepoRoot(_projectPath);
            var gitChangedFiles = repoRoot != null
                ? GitChangeDetector.GetChangedCSharpFiles(repoRoot)
                : null;

            List<FileUsageReport> usageReports;

            var packageNamespaces = new List<string> { _data.packageName ?? "" };

            if (gitChangedFiles != null && gitChangedFiles.Count > 0)
            {
                Console.WriteLine(
                    $"[Pipeline] 🔀 Git mode: scanning {gitChangedFiles.Count} " +
                    "changed file(s) only.");

                usageReports = gitChangedFiles
                    .Select(f =>
                    {
                        // ← Read file content first — Analyze() takes source code string, not a path
                        var sourceCode = File.ReadAllText(f);
                        var roslynResult = RoslynUsageAnalyzer.Analyze(sourceCode, packageNamespaces);

                        // Skip files with no matches
                        if (!roslynResult.ConfirmedUsingLines.Any() &&
                            !roslynResult.FullyQualifiedReferenceLines.Any())
                            return null;

                        // Build a FileUsageReport and enrich it with Roslyn findings
                        var report = new FileUsageReport { FilePath = f, WasGitChanged = true, OriginalContent = sourceCode };
                        RoslynUsageAnalyzer.EnrichReport(report, roslynResult);
                        return report;
                    })
                    .Where(r => r != null)
                    .ToList()!;
            }
            else
            {
                Console.WriteLine(
                    "[Pipeline] 📂 Full scan mode: no git diff available.");

                // ← Recurse folder manually, read each file, then call Analyze()
                usageReports = Directory
                    .GetFiles(projectFolder, "*.cs", SearchOption.AllDirectories)
                    .Select(f =>
                    {
                        var sourceCode = File.ReadAllText(f);
                        var roslynResult = RoslynUsageAnalyzer.Analyze(sourceCode, packageNamespaces);
                        //Print full Roslyn result for this file
                        Console.WriteLine($"\n[Roslyn] ── Report for: {Path.GetFileName(f)} ──");
                        Console.WriteLine($"[Roslyn] Confirmed using lines     : {string.Join(", ", roslynResult.ConfirmedUsingLines)}");
                        Console.WriteLine($"[Roslyn] Qualified ref lines       : {string.Join(", ", roslynResult.FullyQualifiedReferenceLines)}");
                        Console.WriteLine($"[Roslyn] Confirmed namespaces      : {string.Join(", ", roslynResult.ConfirmedNamespaces)}");
                        Console.WriteLine($"[Roslyn] Classes found             : {string.Join(", ", roslynResult.ClassNames)}");
                        Console.WriteLine($"[Roslyn] Methods found             : {string.Join(", ", roslynResult.MethodNames)}");

                        if (!roslynResult.ConfirmedUsingLines.Any() &&
                            !roslynResult.FullyQualifiedReferenceLines.Any())
                            return null;

                        var report = new FileUsageReport { FilePath = f, WasGitChanged = true, OriginalContent = sourceCode };
                        RoslynUsageAnalyzer.EnrichReport(report, roslynResult);
                        return report;
                    })
                    .Where(r => r != null)
                    .ToList()!;
            }

            result.FilesScanned = gitChangedFiles?.Count
                ?? Directory.GetFiles(projectFolder, "*.cs",
                    SearchOption.AllDirectories).Length;
            result.FilesAffected = usageReports.Count;



            result.FilesScanned = gitChangedFiles?.Count
                ?? Directory.GetFiles(projectFolder, "*.cs",
                    SearchOption.AllDirectories).Length;
            result.FilesAffected = usageReports.Count;

            // ── Phase 2: LLM Code Migration ───────────────────────────────────
            Console.WriteLine("\n════════════════════════════════════════");
            Console.WriteLine(
                $"  PHASE 2 — LLM Code Migration ({usageReports.Count} file(s))");
            Console.WriteLine("════════════════════════════════════════");

            var migrator = LLMCodeMigrator.TryCreate(_data);

            if (migrator == null)
            {
                result.FileMigrations = usageReports
                    .Select(r => new FileMigrationResult
                    {
                        FilePath = r.FilePath,
                        RequiresManualReview = true,
                        ManualReviewReason = "LLM not configured.",
                        MigrationSucceeded = false
                    }).ToList();

                result.FilesNeedingManualReview = result.FileMigrations.Count;
            }
            else if (usageReports.Count > 0)
            {
                var migrationResults = await migrator.MigrateFilesAsync(
                    usageReports,
                    _data.packageName ?? "",
                    fromVersion,
                    _data.version ?? "");

                foreach (var m in migrationResults
                    .Where(m => m.MigrationSucceeded
                             && !m.RequiresManualReview
                             && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(m.MigratedContent)))
                {
                    File.Copy(
                        m.FilePath,
                        m.FilePath + ".migration-backup",
                        overwrite: true);

                    await File.WriteAllTextAsync(m.FilePath, m.MigratedContent);
                    Console.WriteLine(
                        $"[Pipeline] ✅ Written: {Path.GetFileName(m.FilePath)}");
                }

                result.FileMigrations = migrationResults;
                result.FilesMigrated = migrationResults
                    .Count(m => m.MigrationSucceeded && !m.RequiresManualReview);
                result.FilesNeedingManualReview = migrationResults
                    .Count(m => !m.MigrationSucceeded || m.RequiresManualReview);
            }
            else
            {
                Console.WriteLine(
                    "[Pipeline] No source files directly use this package's APIs.");
            }

            // ── Phase 2.5: LLM Test Generation ───────────────────────────────
            Console.WriteLine("\n════════════════════════════════════════");
            Console.WriteLine("  PHASE 2.5 — LLM Test Generation");
            Console.WriteLine("════════════════════════════════════════");

            var testGenerator = LLMTestGenerator.TryCreate(_data, _projectPath);
            TestGenerationReport? testReport = null;

            if (testGenerator != null)
            {
                if (usageReports.Count == 0)
                {
                    Console.WriteLine(
                        "[Pipeline] ⚠ Package not used in any source file — " +
                        "test generation skipped.");
                }
                else
                {
                    // ✅ KEY CHANGE: Pass only the files that USE the updated package
                    testReport = await testGenerator.GenerateAndRunAsync(usageReports);
                    result.TestGenerationReport = testReport;
                    Console.WriteLine("\n" + testReport.ToSummaryString());
                }
            }
            else
            {
                Console.WriteLine("[Pipeline] LLM not configured — test generation skipped.");
            }

            // ── Phase 3: Build + self-correction ─────────────────────────────
            Console.WriteLine("\n════════════════════════════════════════");
            Console.WriteLine("  PHASE 3 — Build & Self-Correction");
            Console.WriteLine("════════════════════════════════════════");

            await RunBuildAndTestPhaseAsync(result, projectFolder, migrator);
            if (result.TestsSucceeded && result.TestGenerationReport != null)
            {
                bool phase25WasFailing =
                    !result.TestGenerationReport.BuildSucceeded
                    || result.TestGenerationReport.UnitTestResult?.Passed == false
                    || result.TestGenerationReport.IntegrationTestResult?.Passed == false;

                if (phase25WasFailing)
                {
                    Console.WriteLine(
                        "[Pipeline] ℹ Phase 3 passed — " +
                        "promoting Phase 2.5 report to reflect final outcome.");

                    // Mark the overall build as passed
                    result.TestGenerationReport.BuildSucceeded = true;

                    // Mark unit tests as passed
                    if (result.TestGenerationReport.UnitTestResult != null)
                        result.TestGenerationReport.UnitTestResult.Passed = true;

                    // Mark integration tests as passed
                    if (result.TestGenerationReport.IntegrationTestResult != null)
                        result.TestGenerationReport.IntegrationTestResult.Passed = true;
                }
            }


            return result;
        }

        // ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
        // PUBLIC STATIC — ORCHESTRATION (called from Program.cs)
        // ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

        public static void TryRunNuGetUpdate(RunConfig config)
        {
            var eventPath = Path.Combine(AppContext.BaseDirectory, "event.json");

            if (!File.Exists(eventPath))
            {
                Console.WriteLine("[NuGet] event.json not found. Skipping.");
                return;
            }

            var json = File.ReadAllText(eventPath);

            // Support both single-object { } and array [ { } ] formats
            List<EventData>? dataList = null;
            try
            {
                dataList = System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer
                    .Deserialize<List<EventData>>(json);
            }
            catch
            {
                var single = System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer
                    .Deserialize<EventData>(json);
                if (single != null)
                    dataList = new List<EventData> { single };
            }

            if (dataList == null || dataList.Count == 0)
            {
                Console.WriteLine("[NuGet] event.json empty/invalid. Skipping.");
                return;
            }

            foreach (var data in dataList)
            {
                if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(data.projectPath))
                {
                    Console.WriteLine(
                        "[NuGet] projectPath missing — skipping entry.");
                    continue;
                }

                var fullProjectPath = Path.IsPathRooted(data.projectPath)
                    ? data.projectPath
                    : Path.GetFullPath(
                        Path.Combine(config.LocalRepoPath, data.projectPath));

                Console.WriteLine($"[NuGet] Target: {fullProjectPath}");

                if (!File.Exists(fullProjectPath))
                {
                    Console.WriteLine(
                        $"[NuGet] ⚠ Project file not found: {fullProjectPath}");
                    continue;
                }

                var updater = new NuGetPackageUpdater(data, fullProjectPath);
                updater.UpdatePackage();

                var nugetSummary =
                    $"Updated NuGet packages:{Environment.NewLine}" +
                    $"{data.packageName} -> {data.version}";

                PrContext.GeneratedDescription =
                    string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(PrContext.GeneratedDescription)
                    ? nugetSummary
                    : PrContext.GeneratedDescription
                      + Environment.NewLine + nugetSummary;
            }
        }

        public static void ConvertTableToJSON(
            RunConfig config,
            IConfiguration configuration)
        {
            const string sqlQuery = @"
                SELECT
                    packageName = [package],
                    [version],
                    projectPath
                FROM [PackageUpdater]
                WHERE [update] = 1";

            var packages = new List<EventData>();

            using var connection = new SqlConnection(
                configuration.GetConnectionString("DefaultConnection"));
            using var command = new SqlCommand(sqlQuery, connection);

            try
            {
                connection.Open();
                using var reader = command.ExecuteReader();

                while (reader.Read())
                {
                    packages.Add(new EventData
                    {
                        packageName = reader["packageName"].ToString(),
                        version = reader["version"].ToString(),
                        projectPath = reader["projectPath"].ToString()
                    });
                }

                if (packages.Count > 0)
                {
                    var jsonResult = JsonConvert.SerializeObject(
                        packages, Formatting.Indented);
                    var jsonFilePath = Path.Combine(
                        AppContext.BaseDirectory, "event.json");

                    File.WriteAllText(jsonFilePath, jsonResult);
                    Console.WriteLine(
                        $"[DB] JSON created: {jsonFilePath} " +
                        $"({packages.Count} package(s))");
                }
                else
                {
                    Console.WriteLine("[DB] No packages found for updating.");
                }
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                Console.WriteLine($"[DB] Error: {ex.Message}");
            }
        }

        public static void PowershellNuGetUpdate(RunConfig config)
        {
            var baseDir = AppContext.BaseDirectory;
            var scriptPath = Path.Combine(baseDir, "Scripts", "update-nuget.ps1");
            var eventPath = Path.Combine(baseDir, "event.json");
            var summaryPath = Path.Combine(
                config.LocalRepoPath, "nuget-update-summary.txt");

            if (!File.Exists(scriptPath) || !File.Exists(eventPath)) return;

            var jsonContent = File.ReadAllText(eventPath);
            var packages = JsonConvert.DeserializeObject<List<EventData>>(jsonContent)
                              ?? new List<EventData>();

            var summaryBuilder = new StringBuilder();

            foreach (var package in packages)
            {
                var tempEventPath = Path.Combine(
                    baseDir, $"temp_event_{Guid.NewGuid()}.json");
                var tempSummaryPath = Path.Combine(
                    config.LocalRepoPath, $"temp_summary_{Guid.NewGuid()}.txt");

                File.WriteAllText(tempEventPath,
                    JsonConvert.SerializeObject(package, Formatting.Indented));

                try
                {
                    var psi = new ProcessStartInfo
                    {
                        FileName = "powershell",
                        Arguments =
                            $"-ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File \"{scriptPath}\" " +
                            $"-EventFile \"{tempEventPath}\" " +
                            $"-SummaryFile \"{tempSummaryPath}\" " +
                            $"-RepoRoot \"{config.LocalRepoPath}\"",
                        RedirectStandardError = true,
                        RedirectStandardOutput = true,
                        UseShellExecute = false,
                        CreateNoWindow = true
                    };

                    using var process = new Process { StartInfo = psi };
                    process.Start();

                    var output = process.StandardOutput.ReadToEnd();
                    var error = process.StandardError.ReadToEnd();
                    process.WaitForExit();

                    if (process.ExitCode != 0)
                    {
                        Console.WriteLine(
                            $"[PS] ❌ Failed: {package.packageName} " +
                            $"v{package.version}");
                        Console.WriteLine($"  STDOUT: {output}");
                        Console.WriteLine($"  STDERR: {error}");
                    }
                    else if (File.Exists(tempSummaryPath))
                    {
                        var summary = File.ReadAllText(tempSummaryPath).Trim();
                        if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(summary) &&
                            !summary.Contains("No NuGet package changes"))
                        {
                            summaryBuilder.AppendLine(
                                $"Package: {package.packageName} v{package.version}");
                            summaryBuilder.AppendLine(summary);
                            summaryBuilder.AppendLine();
                        }
                    }
                }
                finally
                {
                    if (File.Exists(tempEventPath)) File.Delete(tempEventPath);
                    if (File.Exists(tempSummaryPath)) File.Delete(tempSummaryPath);
                }
            }

            File.WriteAllText(summaryPath, summaryBuilder.ToString());

            var finalSummary = File.ReadAllText(summaryPath).Trim();
            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(finalSummary) &&
                !finalSummary.Contains("No NuGet package changes"))
            {
                PrContext.GeneratedDescription =
                    string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(PrContext.GeneratedDescription)
                    ? finalSummary
                    : PrContext.GeneratedDescription
                      + Environment.NewLine + finalSummary;
            }
        }

        // ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
        // PRIVATE — BUILD + TEST PHASE
        // ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

        private async Task RunBuildAndTestPhaseAsync(
            MigrationPipelineResult result,
            string projectFolder,
            LLMCodeMigrator? migrator = null)
        {
            var tester = new TestOrchestrator(_projectPath);
            var buildTestResult = await tester.RunAsync();

            result.BuildSucceeded = buildTestResult.BuildSucceeded;
            result.TestsSucceeded = buildTestResult.TestsSucceeded;
            result.BuildOutput = buildTestResult.BuildOutput;
            result.TestOutput = buildTestResult.TestOutput;

            // Self-correction: only when build failed AND LLM is available
            if (!buildTestResult.BuildSucceeded
                && migrator != null
                && buildTestResult.ParsedBuildErrors.Count > 0)
            {
                Console.WriteLine(
                    $"[Pipeline] Build failed " +
                    $"({buildTestResult.ParsedBuildErrors.Count} error(s)). " +
                    "Attempting LLM self-correction...");

                bool corrected = await TrySelfCorrectAsync(
                    migrator, buildTestResult, projectFolder);

                if (corrected)
                {
                    var retryResult = await tester.RunAsync();
                    result.BuildSucceeded = retryResult.BuildSucceeded;
                    result.TestsSucceeded = retryResult.TestsSucceeded;
                    result.BuildOutput +=
                        "\n\n[After self-correction]\n" + retryResult.BuildOutput;
                    result.TestOutput = retryResult.TestOutput;
                }
            }
        }

        // ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
        // PRIVATE — LLM SELF-CORRECTION
        // ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

        private async Task<bool> TrySelfCorrectAsync(
            LLMCodeMigrator migrator,
            TestOrchestrator.BuildTestResult buildResult,
            string projectFolder)
        {
            bool anyCorrected = false;

            var errorsByFile = new Dictionary<string, List<string>>(
                StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

            foreach (var error in buildResult.ParsedBuildErrors)
            {
                // dotnet error format:  src\File.cs(12,5): error CS1234: message
                var match = Regex.Match(error, @"^(.*?\.cs)\(\d+");
                if (!match.Success) continue;

                var file = match.Groups[1].Value.Trim();

                if (!errorsByFile.ContainsKey(file))
                    errorsByFile[file] = new List<string>();

                errorsByFile[file].Add(error);
            }

            foreach (var (filePath, fileErrors) in errorsByFile)
            {
                if (!File.Exists(filePath)) continue;

                var content = await File.ReadAllTextAsync(filePath);
                var errorsText = string.Join("\n", fileErrors);

                var corrected = await migrator.SelfCorrectBuildErrorsAsync(
                    content, errorsText, filePath);

                if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(corrected))
                {
                    await File.WriteAllTextAsync(filePath, corrected);
                    Console.WriteLine(
                        $"[Pipeline] 🔧 Self-corrected: " +
                        $"{Path.GetFileName(filePath)}");
                    anyCorrected = true;
                }
            }

            return anyCorrected;
        }

        // ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
        // PRIVATE — RESTORE HELPERS
        // ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

        /// <summary>
        /// Runs dotnet restore and returns (success, combined output).
        /// Never throws — errors are returned as text for analysis.
        /// </summary>
        private (bool Success, string Output) TryRestore()
        {
            using var process = new Process();
            process.StartInfo = new ProcessStartInfo
            {
                FileName = "dotnet",
                Arguments = $"restore \"{_projectPath}\"",
                RedirectStandardOutput = true,
                RedirectStandardError = true,
                UseShellExecute = false,
                CreateNoWindow = true
            };

            process.Start();
            var stdout = process.StandardOutput.ReadToEnd();
            var stderr = process.StandardError.ReadToEnd();
            process.WaitForExit();

            var combined = stdout + stderr;
            Console.WriteLine(combined);
            return (process.ExitCode == 0, combined);
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Parses NU1605 "downgrade detected" lines from dotnet restore output.
        /// Extracts each conflicting package and the minimum version required.
        ///
        /// NuGet line format:
        ///   EntityAttribute_POC -> PackageName (>= X.Y.Z)
        ///
        /// Returns: Dictionary of { packageName → requiredVersion }
        /// </summary>
        private static Dictionary<string, string> ParseDowngradeConflicts(
            string restoreOutput)
        {
            var result = new Dictionary<string, string>(
                StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

            var lines = restoreOutput.Split(
                new[] { '\r', '\n' }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);

            // Matches:  "-> PackageName.Name (>= X.Y.Z)"
            var pattern = new Regex(
                @"->\s+(?<pkg>[\w\.\-]+)\s+\(>=\s*(?<ver>[\d\.]+)\)",
                RegexOptions.IgnoreCase);

            foreach (var line in lines)
            {
                // Only scan lines that belong to a NU1605 block
                if (!line.Contains("NU1605", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) &&
                    !line.TrimStart().StartsWith("->", StringComparison.Ordinal))
                    continue;

                var match = pattern.Match(line);
                if (!match.Success) continue;

                var pkg = match.Groups["pkg"].Value.Trim();
                var ver = match.Groups["ver"].Value.Trim();

                // Keep the highest required version if the same package appears
                // in multiple conflict lines
                if (!result.TryGetValue(pkg, out var existing) ||
                    CompareVersions(ver, existing) > 0)
                {
                    result[pkg] = ver;
                }
            }

            return result;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Numeric dotted-version comparator.
        /// Returns  1 if a &gt; b,  -1 if a &lt; b,  0 if equal.
        /// Falls back to string comparison for non-standard formats.
        /// </summary>
        private static int CompareVersions(string a, string b)
        {
            try
            {
                return new Version(a).CompareTo(new Version(b));
            }
            catch
            {
                return string.Compare(
                    a, b, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);
            }
        }

        // ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
        // PRIVATE — CSPROJ XML HELPERS
        // ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

        /// <summary>
        /// Reads a PackageReference version directly from the .csproj XML.
        /// Works even when the project has broken restore state (NU1605).
        /// </summary>
        private static string? ReadVersionFromCsproj(
            string projectPath,
            string packageName)
        {
            try
            {
                var xml = XDocument.Load(projectPath);
                var match = xml.Descendants("PackageReference")
                    .FirstOrDefault(pr =>
                        pr.Attribute("Include")?.Value
                          .Equals(packageName,
                              StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) == true);

                return match?.Attribute("Version")?.Value
                    ?? match?.Element("Version")?.Value;
            }
            catch
            {
                return null;
            }
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Returns true if a given package is already listed as a
        /// PackageReference in the .csproj — used to avoid adding
        /// unwanted top-level references during transitive pinning.
        /// </summary>
        private bool IsPackageReferencedInCsproj(string packageName)
        {
            try
            {
                var xml = XDocument.Load(_projectPath);
                return xml.Descendants("PackageReference")
                    .Any(pr =>
                        pr.Attribute("Include")?.Value
                          .Equals(packageName,
                              StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) == true);
            }
            catch
            {
                return false;
            }
        }

        // ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
        // PRIVATE — DOTNET CLI RUNNERS
        // ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

        /// <summary>
        /// Runs a dotnet CLI command and throws on non-zero exit code.
        /// </summary>
        private void RunCommand(string arguments, string errorMessage)
        {
            using var process = new Process();
            process.StartInfo = new ProcessStartInfo
            {
                FileName = "dotnet",
                Arguments = arguments,
                RedirectStandardOutput = true,
                RedirectStandardError = true,
                UseShellExecute = false,
                CreateNoWindow = true
            };

            process.Start();
            var output = process.StandardOutput.ReadToEnd();
            var error = process.StandardError.ReadToEnd();
            process.WaitForExit();

            Console.WriteLine(output);

            if (process.ExitCode != 0)
                throw new Exception($"{errorMessage}\n{output}\n{error}");
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Runs a dotnet CLI command and returns stdout.
        /// Throws on non-zero exit only when errorMessage is provided.
        /// </summary>
        private string RunCommandWithOutput(
            string arguments,
            string errorMessage)
        {
            using var process = new Process();
            process.StartInfo = new ProcessStartInfo
            {
                FileName = "dotnet",
                Arguments = arguments,
                RedirectStandardOutput = true,
                RedirectStandardError = true,
                UseShellExecute = false,
                CreateNoWindow = true
            };

            process.Start();
            var output = process.StandardOutput.ReadToEnd();
            var error = process.StandardError.ReadToEnd();
            process.WaitForExit();

            if (process.ExitCode != 0 &&
                !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(errorMessage))
                throw new Exception($"{errorMessage}\n{output}\n{error}");

            return output;
        }

        // ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
        // PRIVATE — VERSION EXTRACTION FROM dotnet list OUTPUT
        // ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

        /// <summary>
        /// Safely extracts a resolved package version from "dotnet list package"
        /// output. Avoids the original hardcoded [2] index bug.
        ///
        /// Output line format:
        ///   > PackageName    requestedVersion    resolvedVersion
        /// Returns the last token that looks like a version number (x.y.z).
        /// </summary>
        private static string? ExtractVersionFromListOutput(
            string listOutput,
            string packageName)
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(listOutput)) return null;

            var lines = listOutput.Split(
                new[] { '\r', '\n' }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);

            foreach (var line in lines)
            {
                if (!line.Contains(
                        packageName, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
                    continue;

                // Tokens: [">", "PackageName", "requestedVer", "resolvedVer"]
                var tokens = line.Split(
                    ' ', StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);

                // Walk backwards — resolved version is always the last
                // token that starts with a digit and contains a dot
                for (int i = tokens.Length - 1; i >= 0; i--)
                {
                    if (tokens[i].Length > 0
                     && char.IsDigit(tokens[i][0])
                     && tokens[i].Contains('.'))
                        return tokens[i];
                }
            }

            return null;
        }

        // ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
        // PRIVATE — TRANSITIVE DEPENDENCY ALIGNMENT
        // ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

        /// <summary>
        /// Reads project.assets.json to find what versions the updated package
        /// requires transitively, then pins any top-level references that are
        /// behind to avoid NU1605 on the next restore.
        /// </summary>
        private void AlignTransitiveDependencies(
            Dictionary<string, string> updatedPackages)
        {
            var assetsPath = Path.Combine(
                Path.GetDirectoryName(_projectPath)!,
                "obj",
                "project.assets.json");

            if (!File.Exists(assetsPath))
            {
                Console.WriteLine(
                    "[NuGet] project.assets.json not found — " +
                    "skipping dependency alignment.");
                return;
            }

            var assetsJson = File.ReadAllText(assetsPath);
            using var doc = System.Text.Json.JsonDocument.Parse(assetsJson);

            // ── Read top-level deps from project framework section ────────────
            var frameworks = doc.RootElement
                                    .GetProperty("project")
                                    .GetProperty("frameworks");
            var firstFramework = frameworks.EnumerateObject().First();
            var topLevelDeps = firstFramework.Value.GetProperty("dependencies");

            var topLevel = new Dictionary<string, string>(
                StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

            foreach (var pkg in topLevelDeps.EnumerateObject())
            {
                var version = pkg.Value.GetProperty("version")
                                       .GetString() ?? "";
                version = version.Trim('[', ']', '(', ')')
                                 .Split(',')[0].Trim();
                topLevel[pkg.Name] = version;
            }

            // ── Find the resolved dependency tree for the updated package ─────
            var targets = doc.RootElement.GetProperty("targets");
            var firstTarget = targets.EnumerateObject().First();

            System.Text.Json.JsonElement? selectedPackage = null;
            foreach (var pkg in firstTarget.Value.EnumerateObject())
            {
                if (pkg.Name.StartsWith(
                        _data.packageName ?? "",
                        StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
                {
                    selectedPackage = pkg.Value;
                    break;
                }
            }

            if (selectedPackage == null)
            {
                Console.WriteLine(
                    "[NuGet] No dependency tree found for package — " +
                    "skipping alignment.");
                return;
            }

            var resolvedDeps = new Dictionary<string, string>(
                StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

            if (selectedPackage.Value.TryGetProperty(
                    "dependencies", out var deps))
                foreach (var dep in deps.EnumerateObject())
                    resolvedDeps[dep.Name] = dep.Value.ToString();

            // ── Pin any top-level packages whose version is behind ─────────────
            foreach (var top in topLevel)
            {
                if (!resolvedDeps.TryGetValue(
                        top.Key, out var resolvedVersion))
                    continue;

                if (top.Value == resolvedVersion) continue;

                Console.WriteLine(
                    $"[NuGet] Aligning: {top.Key} " +
                    $"{top.Value} → {resolvedVersion}");

                RunCommand(
                    $"add \"{_projectPath}\" package {top.Key} " +
                    $"--version {resolvedVersion} --no-restore",
                    $"Failed to update dependency: {top.Key}");

                updatedPackages[top.Key] = resolvedVersion;
            }

            Console.WriteLine("[NuGet] ✅ Dependency alignment complete.");
        }

    }   // end class NuGetPackageUpdater
}       // end namespace
